basicDetails.schemeName: Mukhyamantri Vidwa Evam Ekal Naari Awas Yojana

nodalDepartmentName.label: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The "Mukhyamantri Vidwa Evam Ekal Naari Awas Yojana" was launched by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Himachal Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial grants for the construction of houses to economically weak widows and single women. Under this scheme, eligible applicants, who are permanent residents of the state will receive financial assistance for constructing a house.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - **An assistance amount of ₹1,50,000/- will be given for the construction of the house.**
schemeContent.briefDescription: The "Mukhyamantri Vidwa Evam Ekal Naari Awas Yojana" was launched by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Himachal Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial grants for the construction of houses to economically weak widows and single women.

children.text: The "Mukhyamantri Vidwa Evam Ekal Naari Awas Yojana" was launched by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Himachal Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial grants for the construction of houses to economically weak widows and single women. Under this scheme, eligible applicants, who are permanent residents of the state will receive financial assistance for constructing a house.
An assistance amount of ₹1,50,000/- will be given for the construction of the house.
Step 1:
The interested applicant downloads the application form
Annexure-1
Step 2:
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3:
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Chief Minister / Chief Minister's office.
Step 4:
Request a receipt (
Annexure-3

) or acknowledgment from the Chief Minister / Chief Minister's office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
Grant Disbursal:
Before payment of the grant amount, the beneficiary will have to give an undertaking on the prescribed form
Annexure-4

applicationProcess.process_md:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant downloads the application form [Annexure-1](http://esomsa.hp.gov.in/sites/default/files/PDF/Scheme%20Mukhyamantri%20Vidwa%20Evam%20Ekal%20Naari%20Awas%20Yojana.pdf). **Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). **Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Chief Minister / Chief Minister's office. **Step 4:** Request a receipt ([Annexure-3](http://esomsa.hp.gov.in/sites/default/files/PDF/Scheme%20Mukhyamantri%20Vidwa%20Evam%20Ekal%20Naari%20Awas%20Yojana.pdf)) or acknowledgment from the Chief Minister / Chief Minister's office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
> **Grant Disbursal:** - Before payment of the grant amount, the beneficiary will have to give an undertaking on the prescribed form [Annexure-4](http://esomsa.hp.gov.in/sites/default/files/PDF/Scheme%20Mukhyamantri%20Vidwa%20Evam%20Ekal%20Naari%20Awas%20Yojana.pdf).

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 1

children.text: Widow” means one whose husband has died and has not remarried.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Widow” means one whose husband has died and has not remarried.

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 2

children.text: Single woman" means a woman (aged 40 years or more), whose husband has been missing for the last seven years, who is unmarried, living separately from her husband or who is currently living with her parents. Be. Apart from this, the Chief Minister of HP will be acceptable for providing age relaxation.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Single woman" means a woman (aged 40 years or more), whose husband has been missing for the last seven years, who is unmarried, living separately from her husband or who is currently living with her parents. Be. Apart from this, the Chief Minister of HP will be acceptable for providing age relaxation.

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 3

children.text: Family” means mother/father, adult/minor son, unmarried daughters whose name is registered with the applicant in the ration card and will come under the ambit of the family.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: Family” means mother/father, adult/minor son, unmarried daughters whose name is registered with the applicant in the ration card and will come under the ambit of the family.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. A widow or a single woman should be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh. 1. The single woman's age should be 40 years or above. 1. The applicant should not have a private residence or house in a dilapidated condition. 1. The applicant's annual family income from all sources does not exceed ₹3,00,000/-. 1. The applicant should have at least 02 Biswa of land registered in the revenue records for construction of house or in whose favor land has been made available by the government. 1. The applicant's family should not receive government assistance for the construction of a house, or the accommodation has not been approved under any other scheme. 1. The grant amount will be approved only once in 10 years provided that the house has not been destroyed due to a natural disaster. 1. Preference will be given to widows and single women on the basis of the following preference: - - Whose houses were destroyed due to natural calamities like fire, flood, earthquake, etc - Suffers from more than 40 percent disability. - All other eligible candidates.

children.text: A widow or a single woman should be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh.
The single woman's age should be 40 years or above.
The applicant should not have a private residence or house in a dilapidated condition.
The applicant's annual family income from all sources does not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
The applicant should have at least 02 Biswa of land registered in the revenue records for construction of house or in whose favor land has been made available by the government.
The applicant's family should not receive government assistance for the construction of a house, or the accommodation has not been approved under any other scheme.
The grant amount will be approved only once in 10 years provided that the house has not been destroyed due to a natural disaster.
Preference will be given to widows and single women on the basis of the following preference: -
Whose houses were destroyed due to natural calamities like fire, flood, earthquake, etc
Suffers from more than 40 percent disability.
All other eligible candidates.
